# UC-22.2.39 · NIS2 OT Incident Detection — Process and Protocol Anomalies

## Description

OT incidents may manifest as Modbus function anomalies, unusual OPC-UA browse depth, or historian gaps rather than Windows malware. This use case baselines industrial protocol traffic per cell line and alerts on deviations that suggest manipulation or sensor spoofing.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes modbus:session. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Build baselines per production cell during golden runs; (2) ingest Modbus/OPC-UA metadata without disrupting real-time control; (3) tune thresholds with engineering; (4) route alerts to OT SOC; (5) feed confirmed incidents into UC-22.2.1/UC-22.2.33 reporting paths. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=modbus:session`; Lookups: `nis2_ot_baseline`, `nis2_ot_baseline.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), HTTP Event Collector (HEC — edge gateway logs).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_protocol` (HEC from Edge Hub or passive taps), `nis2_ot_baseline.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Build baselines per production cell during golden runs; (2) ingest Modbus/OPC-UA metadata without disrupting real-time control; (3) tune thresholds with engineering; (4) route alerts to OT SOC; (5) feed confirmed incidents into UC-22.2.1/UC-22.2.33 reporting paths.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="modbus:session" earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(function_code) as fn_variety avg(register_span) as avg_span by plc_name cell_line
| lookup nis2_ot_baseline.csv cell_line OUTPUT expected_fn_variety expected_span
| eval anomaly=if(fn_variety > expected_fn_variety*2 OR avg_span > expected_span*3, 1, 0)
| where anomaly=1
| table _time, plc_name, cell_line, fn_variety, expected_fn_variety, avg_span, expected_span
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 OT Incident Detection — Process and Protocol Anomalies (Art. 21(2)(f))** — OT incidents may manifest as Modbus function anomalies, unusual OPC-UA browse depth, or historian gaps rather than Windows malware. This use case baselines industrial protocol traffic per cell line and alerts on deviations that suggest manipulation or sensor spoofing.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_protocol` (HEC from Edge Hub or passive taps), `nis2_ot_baseline.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), HTTP Event Collector (HEC — edge gateway logs). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: modbus:session. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="modbus:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **plc_name cell_line**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **anomaly** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where anomaly=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, anomaly, avg_span, cell_line, expected_fn_variety, expected_span, fn_variety, plc_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (function code diversity), Table (anomalous cells), Single value (anomaly count), Heatmap (by shift).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="modbus:session" earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(function_code) as fn_variety avg(register_span) as avg_span by plc_name cell_line
| lookup nis2_ot_baseline.csv cell_line OUTPUT expected_fn_variety expected_span
| eval anomaly=if(fn_variety > expected_fn_variety*2 OR avg_span > expected_span*3, 1, 0)
| where anomaly=1
| table _time, plc_name, cell_line, fn_variety, expected_fn_variety, avg_span, expected_span
```

## Visualization

Line chart (function code diversity), Table (anomalous cells), Single value (anomaly count), Heatmap (by shift).
